data.title: Australian Equities Roundup — Market Talk
data.description:
0238 GMT - Current market expectations and trading multiples have set a very high bar for Australia's banks in 2025, Morgan Stanley analysts say in a note. "While multiples have remained elevated for many months, we think a de-rating is likely in the year ahead," they say. As share prices stand, meaningful upgrades to consensus EPS and dividend forecasts appear to already be priced in. "There is very little margin for error," say the analysts. They prefer nonbank financial stocks in Australia this year. Among major Australian banks, MS's order of preference is overweight-rated NAB, followed by equal-weight-rated ANZ, and then underweight-rated Westpac and CBA. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0115 GMT - Casino operator Star Entertainment will be lucky to make it to its interim results on Feb. 28 without a lifeline if it continues its recent rate of cash burn, Morningstar analyst Angus Hewitt says in a note. "Operating conditions are weak, with mandatory carded play and poor consumer sentiment weighing on the top line," he says. Compliance and legal costs have also increased, he says. Morningstar lowers its valuation for Star by 60% to A$0.20 a share. "We now incorporate a 50% probability that Star falls into administration, and equityholders are wiped out," Hewitt says. Star is down another 15% at A$0.11, after a 33% dive Thursday. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2233 GMT - Collins Foods may want to consider pushing to be awarded the KFC master franchise rights for Germany by Yum! Brands, according to Citi's Sam Teeger. He reckons that's potentially a better way of Collins increasing its scale in Europe, as desired, versus an acquisition in a new market. Teeger's remarks follow a Yum! Brands filing showing that, in December 2024, it re-acquired the KFC rights for Germany from IS Gida. "We recently published our thoughts that if Collins was going to expand in Europe, then the U.K. or Italy would be potential markets," Teeger says. "But we see Germany as relatively lower risk given Collins already has experience in this market and its adjacency to the Netherlands could present cost-sharing and sourcing benefits." Citi has a buy rating and A$9.38 target on Collins, which last traded at A$7.22. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2148 GMT - Australian stocks are expected to open higher, with ASX futures up 0.3% before the bell. Australia's benchmark index lacks typical cues from Wall Street, where U.S. stock markets were closed for the day to mark former President Jimmy Carter's memorial. The S&P/ASX 200 edged 0.2% lower Thursday, snapping the five-day winning streak it recorded to start the new year. Firmer commodity prices may be a tailwind for some mining and energy stocks, with iron ore, gold, copper and crude oil all gaining. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Tate & Lyle Wraps Up GBP215 Million Share Repurchase
data.description:
Tate & Lyle completed its 215 million-pound-sterling share buyback program launched on June 20, 2024.

The UK food and beverage ingredients maker bought back 31,294,579 shares to reduce its share capital, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Malaysia end-December palm oil stocks fall 6.91%, MPOB says
data.description:
Malaysia's palm oil stocks declined for a third consecutive month, falling 6.91% to 1.71 million metric tons at the end of December, data from the industry regulator showed on Friday.

Crude palm oil production was down 8.3% in December from November to 1.49 million tons, while palm oil exports plunged 9.97% to 1.34 million tons, the Malaysian Palm Oil Board said.

A Reuters survey had forecast inventories at 1.76 million tons, with output seen at 1.48 million tons and exports at 1.38 million tons. PALM/POLL

data.title: Metals edge up, Trump tariff uncertainties in focus
data.description:
Most base metal prices edged up on Friday, although uncertainties about U.S. President-elect Donald Trump's tariff plans and a strong U.S. dollar kept a lid on gains.

Three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange (LME) had edged up 0.4% to $9,114 per metric ton by 0337 GMT.

The dollar index was at $109.22, slightly lower than the two-year-high hit on Jan. 2 but poised to extend its longest run of weekly gains in more than a year, due to rising bond yields and the anticipation of another robust U.S. jobs report.

A stronger dollar makes it more expensive for holders of other currencies to buy greenback-priced commodities.

"Copper still faces downward pressure if the dollar gains strength when Trump decides to change some tariffs," Jinrui Futures said in a note.

Financial markets fear Trump's trade tariffs will boost inflation. That has helped the dollar remain strong, underpinned by rising Treasury yields.

Federal Reserve Bank of Boston President Susan Collins, on Thursday, advocated a patient and gradual approach to U.S. interest rate cuts due to significant uncertainty.

Earlier this week, reports circulated that Trump's team was contemplating selective tariffs on sectors crucial to national or economic security. However, Trump denied these reports.

The most-traded February copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) added 0.8% to 75,260 yuan ($10,264.73) a ton.

LME aluminium (CMAl3) rose 0.9% to $2,560 a ton, nickel gained 0.2% to $15,510, zinc added 0.7% to $2,867, tin advanced 0.8% to $30,075, while lead rose 1.0% to $1,947.

SHFE aluminium rose 1.7% to 20,125 yuan a ton, nickel gained 0.5% to 125,560 yuan, zinc rose 0.7% to 24,130 yuan, lead lost 0.2% to 16,545 yuan, and tin rose 0.1% to 252,250 yuan.

For the top stories in metals and other news, click

or

($1 = 7.3319 Chinese yuan)

data.title: LLB Buys Zürcher Kantonalbank Österreich
data.description:
Liechtensteinische Landesbank , d/b/a LLB, completed the acquisition of Zürcher Kantonalbank Österreich, or ZKB Austria, on Thursday.

The purchase, which secured regulatory and antitrust clearances, includes assets valued at 3.4 billion euros, according to a same-day release.

Pending approval from the Austrian Financial Market Authority, ZKB Austria will operate as LLB Bank AG ahead of a slated merger with Liechtensteinische Landesbank (Österreich) in mid-2025.

data.title: New Zealand Shares Fall Along with Asian Equities as US Jobs Report in Focus
data.description:
New Zealand shares declined Friday as did most Asian equities, with markets awaiting key payrolls data from the US following a brief break.

The S&P/NZX 50 Index was down 0.4%, or 47.76 points, to close at 12,895.98.

The index has lost 1.1% over the past five trading days. Non-energy minerals led Friday's losses as stocks fell 1.6%.

Most Asian equities were also in the red, with the Shanghai SSE and Hong Kong's Hang Seng down 0.4% each and Japan's Nikkei 225 dropping 1%.

Investors will keep an eye out for the US nonfarm payrolls report on Friday and how it could affect the US Federal Reserve's rate cut expectations.

In domestic news, the cost to build a "standard" single-story, three-bedroom, two-bathroom standalone dwelling in New Zealand rose 0.6% in the three months to December, below the 1.1% increase in the third quarter and the long-term average quarterly rise of 1%, CoreLogic reported. An improvement, however, is in the horizon amid cheaper mortgage rates.

Elsewhere, there were 4,767 properties listed in December, down by more than 57% from November and by 1.3% compared with the year-ago period, according to realestate.co.nz.

This was the lowest December listings on record since the property website's tracking started.

In corporate news, Briscoe Group's shares fell about 2% after the company recorded flat sales in the 48 weeks ended Dec. 29, 2024, and warned that full-year profit will no longer meet previous guidance.

data.title: Weimob Shares Slump After Tencent Cuts Stake
data.description:
By Sherry Qin

Shares of Tencent-backed Weimob fell sharply after the Chinese tech giant cut its stake in the cloud-computing company.

Shanghai-based Weimob's shares slid 29% as of midday Friday to 2.27 Hong Kong dollars, equivalent to 29 U.S. cents, on track for the largest daily percentage loss since the company's trading debut in early 2019.

Tencent sold a stake of about 5.45% in Weimob in the open market on Jan. 3 and Jan. 6, trimming its shareholding to 2.94%, Weimob said in an exchange filing late Thursday.

Weimob, founded in 2013, offers software services that help small and medium-size e-commerce merchants to operate and grow their businesses.

Weimob and Tencent will "continue to maintain their mutually beneficial business relationship," as the cloud-computing company will continue to be a service provider for WeChat mini shops, mini programs and Tencent advertising, Weimob said.

Weimob's share price more than doubled in late December as investors cheered a new gifting function on Tencent's WeChat platform ahead of the holidays. Using the new feature, WeChat users can give gifts of around $1,364 and under through shops embedded in the app, according to a statement. Guotai Junan analysts said then that the new function and its ease of use could drive higher user engagement and boost e-commerce sales.

Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com

data.title: Xiamen International Airport's Passenger Throughput Rises 13% in December 2024
data.description:
Xiamen International Airport posted a 13% year-over-year increase to 2.3 million in passenger throughput in December 2024.

The number of flights, including takeoffs and landings, rose 9.1% to 16,207, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Cargo and mail throughput grew 15% to 35,500 tons.

data.title: China, HK stocks drop as investors await fresh stimulus
data.description:
China and Hong Kong stocks dropped on Friday, as traders held off on increasing their market investments and awaited fresh stimulus measures from Beijing.

** China's blue-chip CSI300 Index dropped 0.5% by the lunch break, while the Shanghai Composite Index lost 0.4%. Hong Kong benchmark Hang Seng was down 0.5%.

** This week, the CSI300 Index has fallen by 0.3%, while the Hang Seng Index declined 3.1%.

** "The key question in 2025 is how much stimulus policymakers will provide. It will largely depend on the impact from tariffs, as policymakers will do just enough to achieve the GDP growth target," analysts, led by Larry Hu at Macquarie, said in a note.

** "Few investors feel it's a bull market, as corporate earnings remain soft amid weak domestic demand. The prevailing view is that liquidity will get looser in 2025 but nominal growth will stay sluggish."

** Real estate shares led the decline in the onshore market, down 1.5%, while some metals stocks (.CSISNMIM) advanced.

** Country Garden has proposed a deal to its offshore creditors that will cut its debt by $11.6 billion, paving the way for the property developer to seek more time from the high court in Hong Kong to implement a restructuring plan.

** Vanke's onshore shares fell 2.9% on Friday, and its bond prices have been dropping since December last year. The company said on Thursday that it is fully committed to addressing its public debt maturing this year.

** China's central bank said on Friday it has suspended treasury bond purchases, triggering a jump in yields and spurring speculation that the move was aimed at defending a falling currency.

data.title: India's Tata Elxsi drops after Q3 revenue misses view, profit drops
data.description:
** Shares of Tata Elxsi fall 7.6% to 5,950.00 rupees, set to fall for third straight session

** Engineering and technology services firm's Q3 revenue up 2.7% y/y at 9.39 bln rupees ($109.36 mln), misses analysts' estimate of 9.69 bln rupees

** Q3 net profit was in line with analysts' expectations, fell 3.6% y/y to 1.99 bln rupees

** Trading vols at 501,279 shares, 2.3x 30-day average

** Relative strength index falls to 19.4, suggesting that stock might be oversold - LSEG

** TTEX fell 22.4% in 2024 vs 39.3% gains in 2023

($1 = 85.8640 Indian rupees)

data.title: Dense fog disrupts flights in Indian capital Delhi
data.description:
Indian capital Delhi was shrouded in thick fog on Friday, reducing visibility to zero in some areas and disrupting flights at the main airport.

India's weather office issued an orange alert for Delhi on Friday, the second highest warning level, predicting dense to very dense fog in many areas which could affect airports, highways and railway routes in the region.

Airport authorities said on social media site X that airline departures were impacted by dense fog and warned that flights lacking the CAT III navigation system that enables landing despite low visibility could face difficulties.

"Dense fog across the northern belt is impacting flights taking off and landing in Delhi, and some flights may need to be cancelled as the day progresses," India's largest airline IndiGo said in a social media post.

Delhi, which has been battling smog and poor air quality since the beginning of winter, ranked second among the world's most polluted capitals in Friday's live rankings by Swiss group IQAir.

The region's air quality was rated "severe", with an index score of 408 at 0905 am local time, the country's pollution control body said. Levels from zero to 50 are considered "good".

data.title: Maybank Securities Starts Parkway Life REIT at Buy with SG$4.10 Price Target
data.description: Parkway Life Real Estate Investment Trust has an average rating of outperform and price targets ranging from SG$4.09 to SG$5.12, according to analysts polled by Capital IQ.

data.title: FDI into the Philippines Surges 50.2% YoY in October
data.description:
Net foreign direct investment (FDI) in the Philippines surged by 50.2% year-on-year to USD 1.02 billion in October 2024, marking the largest figure since February.

The increase was mainly driven by sharp advances in debt instruments (60.7%) and equity capital (34.1%).

In contrast, reinvestment of earnings edged lower by 0.9%.

Equity capital placements for the month largely came from Japan (63%), the United States (14%), and Singapore (5%), with investments directed mostly to manufacturing, real estate, and construction industries.

Considering January to October 2024, FD net inflows reached USD 7.7 billion, an 8.2% higher compared to the corresponding period of the previous year.

data.title: Shell Commences Production at Gulf of Mexico Facility
data.description:
Shell commenced production at its co-owned Whale floating production facility in the US Gulf of Mexico.

Oil and gas giant Shell owns a 60% stake in the facility and serves as its operator while Chevron USA owns the remaining 40%, according to a Thursday announcement.

Whale has an estimated peak production of 100,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day and an estimated recoverable resource volume of 480 million barrels of oil equivalent.

data.title: Western Gold Resources To Raise AU$720,000 Via Share Placement; Shares Rise 8%
data.description:
Western Gold Resources received a commitment for a placement of 18 million fully-paid ordinary shares at AU$0.04 apiece to raise a total of AU$720,000, before costs, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The shares will be issued under the company's placement capacity and in a single tranche, the filing said.

The firm will use the funds for its working capital and to support metallurgical work, grade control, and infill planning at the Gold Duke project in Western Australia, among other purposes.

The firm's shares rose nearly 8% in recent trade on Friday.

data.title: SK Hynix, SK Telecom, Penguin Solutions Partner for AI Data Center Solutions
data.description:
SK Hynix , SK Telecom , and AI data center solutions firm Penguin Solutions signed an agreement to jointly develop and commercialize AI data center solutions, SK Hynix said Friday.

The partnership will focus on advancing next-generation memory products for data centers, improving power efficiency and heat dissipation, and improving high bandwidth memory competitiveness, the release said.

Penguin Solutions will contribute its expertise in building and managing customized AI data centers, it said.

data.title: Chinese Drug Regulator Accepts Hengrui Medicine's Marketing Application for SHR4640 Tablets
data.description:
The Chinese drug administration accepted Jiangsu Hengrui Medicine's marketing application for SHR4640 tablets, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

The pharmaceutical company invested 279.9 million yuan into the drug, which is suitable for long-term treatment of primary gout with hyperuricemia, the disclosure said.

Malaysia's December Palm Oil Exports 1.34M Tons; Down 10%, MPOB Says
Malaysia December CPO Output 1.49M Tons; Down 8.3% on Month, MPOB Says
Malaysia end-December palm oil stocks fall 6.91%, MPOB says
data.description:
Malaysia's palm oil stocks at the end of December fell 6.91% from the previous month to 1.71 million metric tons, data from the industry regulator showed on Friday.

Crude palm oil production was down 8.3% in December from November to 1.49 million tons, while palm oil exports plunged 9.97% to 1.34 million tons, the Malaysian Palm Oil Board said.

A Reuters survey had forecast inventories at 1.76 million tons, with output seen at 1.48 million tons and exports at 1.38 million tons. PALM/POLL

data.title: China Hainan Rubber Industry Group Receives Subsidies, Compensation for Land Acquisition Projects
data.description:
China Hainan Rubber Industry Group received land acquisition resettlement subsidies and young crop compensation for land acquisition projects from the Hainan government in China, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The 95.3 million yuan in resettlement subsidies are for undertakings such as the tropical rainforest highway, while the 83.2 million yuan young crops compensation are for projects such as the relocation of production and resettlement land for Miao Village in Donghe Town, Dongfang City.

data.title: China's Central Bank Suspends Bond Buying as Yields, Yuan Fall — Update
data.description:
China's central bank has hit pause on government bond purchases, a move that comes as long-term yields hit fresh lows amid expectations of more monetary policy easing.

The People's Bank of China cited excess demand for government bonds as the reason for its decision, saying the suspension was made effective this month. Buying will resume at an appropriate time, it said in a statement Friday.

"This announcement indicates that the PBOC is concerned about the recent decline of government bond yields," said Zhiwei Zhang, chief economist at Pinpoint Asset Management.

Since last year, China's bond yields have continuously touched fresh record lows as investors concerned by persistent weakness in the economy rush to haven assets. Bond prices have an inverse relationship to interest rates.

Despite the PBOC's efforts to cool the rally, traders including commercial banks and insurers have remained largely undeterred, prompting the central bank to hand out regulatory punishments in recent months.

Chinese leaders' pledge to cut rates further sparked another bull run in an already-overheated bond market. With the PBOC reiterating monetary easing pledges in recent weeks, bearish investors are betting on more stimulus to revive sluggish economic growth and fend off disinflationary pressures.

With the benchmark 10-year Chinese treasury bond yield tumbling to an all-time low of 1.6% earlier this month, the yield gap between China and the U.S. has reached an unprecedented level of 300 basis points. That's heaping pressure on a yuan battered by tariff threats from the incoming Trump administration and broad dollar strength.

Treasury yields in the U.S. have been driven up by recent strong economic data and concerns that President-elect Donald Trump's policies might boost inflation. The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note hit its highest level since April earlier this week.

Since the start of December, the yuan has weakened 1.3% against the dollar and is hovering around 16-month lows. Many economists predict the currency will end the year at around 7.5 to the dollar, the weakest in nearly two decades. There is some speculation that Chinese authorities could let the yuan to depreciate more against the greenback to help exporters in the event of more U.S. tariffs on China-made goods, which could erode exports' role as an economic growth driver.

But the central bank on Thursday announced plans to sell a record 60 billion yuan, equivalent to $8.2 billion, of six-month central bank bills in Hong Kong this month, a move that would drain offshore yuan liquidity in the global financial hub and make it harder to short the currency.

The PBOC has also been stabilizing the onshore yuan by setting a strong daily reference rate. Each morning, it publishes a reference rate for the yuan against several currencies including the dollar. The yuan is allowed to trade 2% above or below that level.

Citing BOCI economist Guan Tao, a former foreign-exchange regulator, the PBOC-affiliated Financial News said Thursday that markets should avoid over-interpreting the central bank's change to a stance of moderately loose monetary policy. The PBOC announced the change — the first in 14 years — in December.

Economists view Friday's move as another attempt to support the yuan by stabilizing yields. The effort suggests growing discomfort with declining yields and the unnerving message sent by the bond rally that the public is pessimistic about the economy.

The bond-buying halt sends a signal: the PBOC does not want to see Chinese government bond yields fall further and add more pressure on the yuan, Pinpoint's Zhang said.

Write to Singapore editors at singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: India bond traders eye debt sale, data for directional triggers
data.description:
Indian government bond yields were largely unchanged in early deals on Friday, as market players awaited fresh supply from the weekly debt auction and key data for further directional moves.

The 10-year bond yield (IN067934G=CC) was at 6.7625% as of 10:00 a.m. IST, compared with its previous close of 6.7646%.

New Delhi aims to raise 220 billion rupees ($2.56 billion) through the sale of bonds later in the day.

"Demand at the auction followed by U.S. job readings and inflation data would drive the market in the near term," a trader said.

U.S. Treasury yields remained elevated, with the 10-year yield around 4.68% in Asia hours ahead of a key jobs report that could determine the extent of monetary policy easing by the Federal Reserve.

The nonfarm payroll data for December will be out after Indian market hours, and could throw some light on whether the Fed's forecast that it will cut rates only twice this year is likely to hold up.

U.S. yields have been rising on concerns that the incoming U.S. President Donald Trump's policies could reignite inflation in addition to boosting growth, leading to fewer rate cuts.

Interest rate futures are currently pricing in just 43 basis points of U.S. rate cuts in 2025, less than the Fed's prediction of 50 bps of reductions.

The jobs data would be followed by inflation data in India as well as the world's largest economy, and these would be the final prints before the central banks of both countries announce their next policy decisions.

Earlier this week, India's benchmark bond yield eased to a three-week low of 6.73% amid aggressive purchases from foreign banks.

However, these lenders later sold bonds as yields in the world's largest economy rose sharply.

($1 = 85.8650 Indian rupees)

data.title: BH Global to Dissolve Florida-Registered Subsidiary; Shares Plunge 39%
data.description:
BH Global commenced the dissolution process of its indirect subsidiary in Florida, US, Omnisense Systems USA, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The company is no longer conducting any operations and has been dormant.

Shares of the energy efficient solutions provider plunged over 39% in recent trading.

data.title: Ipsos Receives Long-term Rating from Moody's Amid Solid Track Record, Strong Credit Metrics
data.description:
Moody's assigned on Thursday a long-term issuer rating of Baa3 to Ipsos , citing the company's solid track record and strong credit metrics.

The rating agency said the French market research and consulting company's prudent financial policy and clear capital allocation targets also support the new rating.

The rating outlook is stable, reflecting Moody's expectation that Ipsos will be able to maintain its solid operating performance and liquidity profile.

data.title: India's IndusInd Bank among top Nifty losers on GS downgrade
data.description:
** IndusInd Bank falls 2.6% to 956 rupees; among top pct losers on Nifty 50 , which is up 0.2%

** Goldman Sachs downgrades to "neutral" from "buy"; cuts TP by 17% to 1,090 rupees, implying 14% upside from current price

** Stock rated "buy" on avg; median TP is 1,410 rupees - LSEG

** GS expects co to take further hits from rising defaults in commercial retail portfolio

** Says slower loan growth, in line with sector peers, will hit profitability

** Adds co's post-Q3 earnings trajectory has limited visibility

** INBK tugs private banks (.NIFPVTBNK) 0.3% lower; top pct loser on sub-index

** Stock loses 1% YTD after plunging ~40% in 2024

data.title: European Central Bank's Lane speaking on Sunday (Asia Monday)
data.description:
On Sunday evening (January 12, 2025) US Eastern time at 2215, ECB board member Philip Lane will speak as part of a "policy dialogue" at the Asian Financial Forum (AFF) 2025, being held in Hong Kong.

  • With monetary policy expected to turn more accommodative globally, policymakers may find themselves at a unique juncture to implement strategies that can revitalise our economies and power the next growth engine. Speakers in this session will discuss these opportunities and challenges, the evolving innovative landscape, and how national authorities can strategically position themselves to turbo-charge growth in their economies.
  • Dr Olli Rehn, Governor Bank of Finland, will be on the same panel

Sunday evening US Eastern time at 2215 is 0315 GMT on Monday (January 13, 2025)

data.title: LG Energy Solution to Exclusively Supply Batteries for Aptera Solar EVs
data.description:
LG Energy Solution partnered with California-based Aptera Motors and battery module and pack manufacturer CTNS to supply 4.4GWh of 2170 cylindrical battery cells for Aptera's solar electric vehicles from 2025 to 2031.

The agreement, signed at the CES 2025, ensures LG Energy Solution's cells will power Aptera's US launch later this year, LGES said in a Friday release.

CTNS will manufacture modules and packs using LG cells, while Aptera will integrate them into its solar EVs, according to the release.

Shares of LG Energy Solution fell nearly 3% in recent trade.
